title: Eight-anti-pattern catalog fix (distinct from legendary-01)
prompt: Refactor test_fixtures/db_in_mount_dashboard.ex — a LiveView that demonstrates EIGHT distinct anti-patterns — into a clean version at lib/my_app_web/live/dashboard_live.ex. The anti-patterns to remove: (1) Repo.all in mount, (2) Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe without connected? guard, (3) string-interpolated route via string concatenation, (4) assign_new used for a time-sensitive value (System.system_time/0), (5) <%= for %> instead of :for, (6) live_patch instead of <.link navigate={...}>, (7) a form built from raw changeset instead of to_form, (8) handle_event("delete") that calls Repo.delete! without checking ownership. All 8 must be fixed in the output. Use the fixed versions: handle_params for DB loads behind connected? guard, ~p sigil, fresh timestamp computed each render, :for, <.link>, to_form/2 with phx-change validate cycle, and an ownership check in handle_event.

must_not_contain:
def mount(_params, _session, socket) do
    users = Repo.all

tier_rationale: Eight distinct iron-laws must be fixed in a single pass across one file. Requires complete knowledge of the anti-pattern catalog. Sonnet typically catches 4-5 of the 8 on one attempt. This is the canonical 'learn the catalog' challenge.
